title: Service-animal evacuation and access kit
summary: A paired-person plan for identification, food, water, medicines, waste care, safe transport, task continuity, and shelter communication without separating the service animal unnecessarily.
content:
A paired-person plan for identification, food, water, medicines, waste care, safe transport, task continuity, and shelter communication without separating the service animal unnecessarily.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
materials: leash harness carrier or other usual control equipment
steps: Prepare the kit around the individual team: normal food, water, medicines, waste supplies, bedding, records, veterinarian, emergency contact, and backup handler instructions.
Mark the animal and equipment clearly, practise transport and alternative relief locations, and keep task or command information private except where needed for safety.
At evacuation tell staff that the animal is a service animal, describe space or access needs, and keep the team together unless immediate medical or safety conditions require a temporary alternative.
After exposure to smoke, heat, floodwater, debris, or injury, obtain veterinary advice and inspect paws, breathing, hydration, behaviour, and equipment before resuming work.
warnings: Do not distract, feed, handle, or separate a working service animal without permission.
Never leave an animal in a hot, cold, smoky, or unventilated vehicle.
common_mistakes: Starting Service-animal evacuation and access kit without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Service-animal evacuation and access kit.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Service-animal evacuation and access kit when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Service-animal evacuation and access kit when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Service-animal evacuation and access kit.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Service-animal evacuation and access kit safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Service-animal evacuation and access kit when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Service-animal evacuation and access kit.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Service-animal evacuation and access kit should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Service-animal evacuation and access kit whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Service-animal evacuation and access kit in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Service-animal evacuation and access kit after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Service-animal evacuation and access kit cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Service-animal evacuation and access kit are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Service-animal evacuation and access kit to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Service-animal evacuation and access kit.']
